channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Murdoch-linked MacLeod family gains influence in climate-focused political funding
item.description: The article examines the rising political and environmental influence of the MacLeod family, who are connected to the Murdoch media dynasty through Prudence Murdoch and her husband Alasdair MacLeod. I...

item.title: Investigations Continue as No Arrests Are Made in Kubheka Kidnapping
item.description: Gauteng police have confirmed that there have been no arrests in the case of Mazwi Kubheka, the Vosloorus spaza shop owner who was kidnapped and held for a month. Police Commissioner Tommy Mthombeni s...

item.title: Tanzania strengthens digital skills and technology partnership with China
item.description: Tanzania has shown strong commitment to deepen its technological cooperation with China as part of advancing the country’s digital transformation agenda. Speaking during the Experience China High-Tech...

item.title: Residents Allowed to Return After Fatal House Explosion in Bristol
item.description: Residents in a Bristol neighbourhood have been allowed to return to their homes following a fatal explosion that left two people dead and three others injured. The incident occurred at a property on S...

item.title: Lung Recovery After Quitting Smoking: What Science Says
item.description: Recent research indicates that human lungs have a significant ability to repair themselves after damage, including damage caused by smoking. Dr Charlotte Dean, from Imperial College London, explains t...

item.title: Headinvest LLC Increases Holdings in SPDR Gold Shares by 16.1%
item.description: Headinvest LLC has increased its holdings in SPDR Gold Shares (NYSEARCA:GLD) by 16.1% during the fourth quarter, adding 2,741 shares to reach a total of 19,769 shares. These shares now represent 1.6% ...

item.title: Iranian Red Crescent Submits War Crime Evidence Against US and Israel to ICC
item.description: The Iranian Red Crescent Society (IRCS) has officially submitted evidence of alleged war crimes by the United States and Israel to the International Criminal Court (ICC) and other international bodies...

item.title: Mercy Wanjiru: Friend Who Invited Her to Party Seeks Forgiveness Ahead of Burial
item.description: Mercy Wanjiru, a 23‑year‑old entrepreneur from Ruai, Nairobi, died days after being seriously injured following a night out at a party that she had been invited to by friends. Reports indicate she was...

item.title: US to assist stranded ships in Strait of Hormuz amid regional tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced say America go start helping ships wey don get stuck for Gulf due to ongoing conflict between US-Israel and Iran. Some tanker reports show say them don get hit by u...

item.title: The Rise of Subliminal Audio for Self-Image and Confidence
item.description: A growing number of young women are turning to subliminal videos on TikTok and YouTube to improve their appearance and boost self-confidence. These videos often feature distorted audio affirmations, A...

item.title: OpenAI Adds Custom AI Pets to Codex for Developer Assistance
item.description: OpenAI has introduced a new feature to its Codex app, adding customizable AI-generated pets to enhance user interaction. These animated companions do not participate in coding but serve as floating ov...

item.title: Dr. Oz Leads CMS Efforts to Address Medicare and Medicaid Fraud
item.description: Dr. Mehmet Oz, widely known for his television career as “America’s Doctor,” now serves as the administrator of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) under the Trump administration. He ...

item.title: Family urges Saskatchewan to implement consistent diabetes care standards in schools
item.description: A Saskatchewan family is calling on the provincial government to introduce a standardized approach to caring for students with diabetes in schools, arguing that current practices vary widely and can p...

item.title: RCMP Continue Search for Missing American Tourist at Banff Waterfall
item.description: Authorities in Alberta are continuing efforts to locate the body of an American tourist who went missing near a waterfall in Banff National Park. The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) confirmed tha...

item.title: A selection of the most impactful daily images from AP photojournalists
item.description: This article presents a curated gallery of the day's most striking photographs, chosen by editors at the Associated Press. The images span a wide range of subjects, from breaking news events to moment...

item.title: Lahore High Court temporarily suspends Meesha Shafi’s Rs5m defamation penalty to Ali Zafar
item.description: On Monday, the Lahore High Court (LHC) granted a conditional suspension of a sessions court verdict which had ordered singer Meesha Shafi to pay Rs5 million in damages to fellow singer and actor Ali Z...

item.title: Poor mobile coverage in Tenby disrupts businesses and frustrates visitors
item.description: Tenby, a popular seaside town in Pembrokeshire, Wales, is facing significant challenges due to poor mobile phone coverage, affecting both residents and the large number of tourists who visit each year...

item.title: Over 133,000 Students Take ACSEE Exams and Teacher Training Tests in Tanzania
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Today, Tanzania sees 133,655 candidates sitting for the 2026 Advanced Certificate of Secondary Education Examination (ACSEE), covering both Mainland Tanzania and Zanzibar. Of these, 12...

item.title: King Charles’s US visit reflects political decline and uncertainty in the UK and US
item.description: The article argues that King Charles III’s 2026 state visit to the United States symbolises a broader moment of রাজনৈতিক and cultural decline in both the UK and the US. Nesrine Malik frames the visit ...

item.title: GLP-1 Medication Semaglutide Shows Association with Reduced Depression and Anxiety
item.description: A large-scale study conducted by the University of Eastern Finland, Karolinska Institutet in Sweden, and Griffith University in Australia has found that GLP-1 receptor agonists, including semaglutide ...
